author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text:
Oh boy... The purpose of life is to BE. Therefore life IS . By extension , the purpose of life is to continue to BE , therefore (because no life is eternal , as far as I know) , it's purpose is to extend itself (re-procreate).  All the rest is circumstantial , for all living things.
 If I didn't watched the video and if you were talking in an American , little bit drawn out accent (instead of smart British one) , I would be imagining some long haired hippie in late 60's  , with hair ribbon and round colored glasses , in flowered shirt , while exhaling some weed smoke..."Man...the purpose of life is to be alive...".

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: Tathagata, and I quote "The term is often thought to mean either "one who has thus gone" (tathā-gata), "one who has thus come" (tathā-āgata), or sometimes "one who has thus not gone" (tathā-agata). This is interpreted as signifying that the Tathāgata is beyond all coming and going – beyond all transitory phenomena." a Buddhist notion. However i find it heartbreaking but perhaps this is just a physical phenomenon of my body deceiving me

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: The transforming experience of True Reality being "suchness" and as they say, Thou Art That - Nice pun, the "transforming" experience! However my father's struck fear in my heart as I lost my composure doing network admin without internship or continuity. I know I will become homeless. I'm not sure what I'll do, but I wanted to work, not be a wanderer without purpose. Exposure to the totally random and no long term goal has made me truly hopeless. My 81 year old father's a still-coding computer scientist and has no interest in even conversing with me. It's destroyed my capacity to make meaningful connections with anyone. I feel what gives us a SENSE of purpose is that feeling of connection and belonging, not a feeling of alienation and being driven away. I'm terrified of him yet love him dearly, I tried to flee to England to apply for asylum there, as i would do any work there, there is public transport and I wish to work.not be a stray wanderer, I who wished to neither stray nor wander. I am truly frightened and hope to die before my father does (without being aware of dying), my siblings do not realise that I cannot take care of myself and have turned a deaf ear for so many years. I feel that i have gone, my thoughts are fragmented and not systemic I've no peace and my father has pushed me to the edge where I live in terror. https://www.perplexity.ai/search/Hello-I-would-yvY3vIY7RVCBDgvWYAmuAw?s=c

author: @frankbenevolence
text:
Purpose is often framed in terms of predestination, what you're "supposed" to do according to some divine plan. This is by definition illusory, but purpose in terms of intention is perfectly reasonable, highly recommended, and in some ways unavoidable.  If you do something because you enjoyed it in the past, is not the purpose of repeating the action to enjoy yourself?
We have much greater capacity for mental time travel and can project our goals further into the future, but the difference between the mental capacities of a cat is a matter of degree not of kind.  
The jury is still out for level of intentionality displayed by plants,  but animals clearly have an ability to predict consequences which is not entirely unlike our own, if less sophisticated.

author: @VeniceInventors
text:
While I agree that as humans we don't have to have a purpose in our society, my understanding is that all living organisms, humans included, have a purpose in nature. Accordingly our purpose is to pick and eat fruit and drop the seeds or kernels some distance away from the tree or bush they came from, helping their offsprings to be spread out. Basically the same as any ape, most other mammals and birds.  Any activity beyond that being completely optional.
Of course in the society the maddest of inbred humans have created for the rest of us, it's not that simple anymore, and we are expected to agree to one form of servitude or another, for the benefit of the few still clinging to that lineage of madness.

text: I would like to say that I live my life in the moment truly not knowing what the next day will bring, and in a way thats true, oh yes I do plan things ahead, book holidays etc, but even when planned I try and take each day as it comes and try out new experiences when the opportunity presents, but am also happy just to spend an entire day relaxing and doing nothing (other than what inspiration and events present along the way. (if it involves a modicum of tinkering en route then all the better).

author: @totherarf
text:
As I approach retirement I find myself thinking about this more often.

When talking with people I am often asked, "What do you do?". I find that concept in itself interesting. What they really mean is what do you do for a living, in order to earn money and by definition have the expertise in. I have several different answers.
I play with the Grey Stuff is probably the most accurate one but do have others ...... I am a generalist (as opposed to a specialist) I am an Archer, Pilot collector of Sci-fi ..... but I get paid for playing with Big Electric!

Back in the '70's it used to be a thing that you worked (if you were lucky) from 17 to 65 retired, got a pension, lived on average a further 2 years and died! I do Not subscribe to that viewpoint! 

Life is too short. I expect to fill it to the brim and enjoy as much of it as I can without encountering ennui!

replies.author: @orpheuscreativeco9236
replies.text: I starting realizing how loaded a question that was in my twenties and began to answer with a split answer. I'm now on the quest for that combination of joy meets perceived value, joy being the primary state 👍 I appreciate the generalist approach as well.

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text:
@orpheuscreativeco9236 The biggest problem I find is balance. Having enough to be comfortable with enough of a buffer so unforeseen things are not too much of a worry ..... whilst at the same time not eather grabbing every opportunity to earn £ or to slide up the greasy pole of promotion or outperform all the neighbours!
Sometimes that is hard, and I do realise that in itself is a first world problem!
